title: aquilaria citrinicarpa
intro: Aquilaria citrinicarpa, also known as the 'bois deoudou' or 'black resin tree', is a species of tree native to the tropical regions of Australia, Indonesia, and Papua New Guinea. It is a type of agarwood tree that is prized for its unique, fragrant resin, which forms as a defense mechanism against fungal infections. The resin of Aquilaria citrinicarpa has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, and is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the resin is often used to make essential oils, incense, and perfumes, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
